$comment: Config for submit-plugin.mjs — the OpenAI plugin submission automation. Edit these values to reuse the automation for any MCP plugin. Secrets (the reviewer demo password) are NOT stored here; they are read from ../.env.local by env var name.

info.name: NotesBridge
info.subtitle: Apple Notes in ChatGPT
info.subtitleNote: Subtitle MUST be 30 characters or fewer (OpenAI validates this).
info.description: NotesBridge connects ChatGPT to the notes on your Mac. Search and read any note, create new ones, append to lists, or rewrite drafts — all from a chat. Your notes never live on our servers: every action is executed on your own Mac by a small open-source agent you install with one command (npx apple-notes-agent), and the relay only carries each request for the seconds it's in flight. Write actions are always confirmed by you in ChatGPT before they happen. Open source (MIT) and self-hostable.
info.developerIdentityNote: Substring matched against the verified identities in the dropdown. Requires completing developer identity verification first.
info.demoRecordingNote: REQUIRED by OpenAI. A recorded demo (NotesBridge listing folders, creating a note, reading it back in ChatGPT) is hosted here. Swap for a YouTube link if preferred.

reviewerDemo.note: Reviewers use these to test the app 24/7 with no Mac (demo mode). The password lives in ../.env.local, never in this file.

testPrompts.expected: search → 'Grocery list', then fetch → milk, eggs, coffee beans, blueberries

testPrompts.prompt: Create a note titled Demo idea with the body Try NotesBridge
testPrompts.expected: create_note → confirms, returns the new note

testPrompts.prompt: List the notes in my Work folder
